summary: LeadStoke sells done-for-you and DIY lead-generation programs to local small businesses (restaurants, retail, salons/spas, fitness, and home-service trades) under founder Dr. Jay Herrera. Its programs — Yelp Help, Review Tap, Birthday Marketing Formula, Coupon Club, and email/SMS marketing — help owners build a customer list they own and bring people back, sold month-to-month with no contracts and a free trial so clients pay only after they see results.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Get a new free-trial signup to their first tangible result — a fresh review, a redeemed birthday coupon, or a customer who came back — before the trial window closes.
campaigns.segment: New trial users; branch by program started (Review Tap, Birthday Marketing Formula, Coupon Club) and business type.
campaigns.rationale: LeadStoke's whole model is 'try before you buy,' so the trial-to-first-result gap is the single highest-risk moment — owners who don't see a quick early win never convert to paid.

emails.purpose: Warm, founder-voice welcome that sets expectations and gets the owner to do the one setup step that produces their first review or lead.
emails.subject: You're in — let's get your first review this week
emails.preview: One 3-minute setup step and the results start rolling in.
keyPoints: Hi from Jay — you signed up to build a customer list you actually own, so let's not waste your time getting there
The single first action (connect your profile / send your first review request) takes about 3 minutes and is what kicks off your first real result
Set the expectation: most owners see a new review or lead inside the first week — and you don't pay a dime until you do
No agency jargon, no contracts — just reply to this email and a real person (me) answers

campaigns.name: Trial-to-Paid Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert an engaged free-trial user into a paying monthly client by showing the result they already got and handling the 'I've been burned by agencies' objection.
campaigns.trigger: Trial activation event (first review/lead generated) plus trial-period progress
campaigns.segment: Active trials; branch by activated (saw a result) vs. stalled (no result yet).
campaigns.rationale: Converting free-trial users to paying monthly clients is LeadStoke's stated primary goal and core revenue event, and the proof is already in their own results — no discount needed.

emails.purpose: Reflect the owner's own trial results back at them, tie it to monthly revenue, and make staying on as easy as a reply — directly answering the 'agencies burned me' fear.
emails.subject: You just got 4 new reviews — here's what that's worth
emails.preview: This is the system working. Here's how to keep it running.
keyPoints: Show their actual trial numbers (reviews collected, coupons redeemed, customers reached) — proof the system works for THEIR business, not a case study
Connect those wins to repeat revenue: more reviews mean more people finding you, more redemptions mean customers coming back
Handle the #1 objection head-on: month-to-month, no contract, cancel anytime — the opposite of the agency that locked you in and disappeared
Low-friction next step: reply or grab a 10-minute call with Jay to switch on the paid program

campaigns.name: Consult Booking & Reminders
campaigns.objective: Maximize attended consultation calls with Jay and recover the ones that slip, so high-intent owners actually get on the phone.
campaigns.trigger: Consultation call booked; upcoming call; no-show event
campaigns.segment: Anyone who booked a call with Jay (trial setup, results review, or program questions).
campaigns.rationale: Deals close on a founder-led phone call with Jay, so a missed call is a missed sale — reminders cut no-shows ~35% and protect the brand's main close mechanism.

emails.purpose: Remind the owner of tomorrow's call, set a clear agenda so it feels worth their time, and make rescheduling one click instead of a no-show.
emails.subject: See you tomorrow — quick call about your customer list
emails.preview: 15 minutes with Jay. Here's what we'll cover.
emails.cta: Confirm or pick a better time
keyPoints: Confirm the day and time and that it's a real conversation with Jay, not a sales script
Set the agenda: review your trial results, answer your questions, and map which program brings customers back fastest for your type of business
Reassure on time: it's about 15 minutes and there's no obligation and no contract
One-click reschedule link so a conflict becomes a new time instead of a missed call

campaigns.name: Free-Trial Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move a hesitant trial signup who hasn't fully engaged toward their first result and sales-readiness over a short cycle.
campaigns.trigger: Trial started but key setup step not completed
campaigns.segment: Signed-up trial users who haven't activated; branch by program and business type.
campaigns.rationale: Many owners start the free trial then stall because 'marketing feels complicated' — a warm nurture that removes friction is what turns a dormant trial into a paying client.

emails.purpose: Re-engage a stalled trial by shrinking the perceived effort and showing a peer business owner who got results without the marketing headache.
emails.subject: Marketing felt complicated? This part takes 3 minutes
emails.preview: How a salon down the street filled its slow days — without an agency.
keyPoints: Meet the owner where they are: you're busy and you've heard 'marketing' a hundred times — this isn't that
Short peer story: a local business like theirs got their first wave of reviews/repeat customers from one simple setup step
Remove the friction — point them to the single 3-minute action that starts results, and offer to do it for them on a quick call
Reinforce zero risk: still free, still no contract, you only pay once it works

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate lapsed trial users and cancelled clients, and clean the list to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: Inactivity threshold (no engagement 60–90 days) or program cancellation
campaigns.segment: Dormant trial signups who never converted; separately, churned clients (different message referencing what they used).
campaigns.rationale: Lapsed trials and cancelled month-to-month clients are a recoverable, low-cost segment — and a clean re-engagement pass protects sender reputation for the active list.

emails.purpose: A warm, no-pressure 'we miss you' that reminds the owner of the core value and what's new, with an easy door back in.
emails.subject: Still want more customers coming back?
emails.preview: It's Jay. No pitch — just an easy way back if you're ready.
emails.cta: Pick up where you left off
keyPoints: Plain, human check-in from Jay — no guilt, just a reminder of what they came for: a customer list they own and repeat business
Show what's changed since they left (a new program like Birthday Marketing Formula, or an easier setup)
Make the return frictionless — one click to restart the free trial, still no contract and no cost until it works
Respect their inbox: if it's not the right time, the next email will ask if they'd like to stop hearing from us
